H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (Pebble, n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, strong confidence) is the second-most sampled protocol with robust right-weighted distribution (mode at 8, 26 scores; 17 scores at 9-10). Qualitative (n=29) shows 'Noticeable improvement' (12), balanced music/rhythm, and diverse activity contexts. YouTube reach is highest in dataset (views=30) but retention data is NULL — this is the most 'discovered' protocol but entrainment completion is unknown. AutoGluon predicts 6.859, below observed, flagging tension between reach and efficacy prediction.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble is the most popular over-stimulation protocol with strong results; most listeners notice improvement with a balanced, calming rhythm that works for many activities.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (Rainfall, n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, strong confidence) shows slight dip from L1, with distribution mass at 7-8 (25/51). Qualitative data shows 'Noticeable improvement' (10) as primary signal and 'Balanced / comfortable' rhythm consensus (7). YouTube views are low (5), making this protocol relatively undiscovered despite solid scores. Embedding clusters tightly with L3 (sim=0.786).
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall offers reliable over-stimulation relief with noticeable improvement and a comfortable rhythm, though it's not widely discovered yet.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (Glacier, n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, meaningful confidence) recovers slightly over L2 with tight variance — the most consistent Over-stimulation performer. Qualitative data reveals unique activity mode: 'Trying to sleep' (8/23), suggesting off-label usage. Rhythm opinion splits evenly between 'Balanced' and 'Very settling' (10 each). This off-label pattern may explain tight variance — sleep-seekers form a more homogeneous population.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier has solid performance and seems to work especially well for people using it to fall asleep — the rhythm is balanced with a very settling quality for many.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H-AX-L1 (Meadow, n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, strong confidence) is the highest-volume HEALING protocol with solid efficacy. Distribution is right-weighted with 14 scores at 9 and 7 at 10, though wide std indicates tail variance. Qualitative data (n=23) shows 'Myself' listeners (13) dominating, with 'Balanced / comfortable' rhythm opinion (13) and predominantly 'Moderate / Noticeable improvement' (18). YouTube reach is minimal (views=7) with NULL retention. AutoGluon predicts 7.838 for the anxiety zone, closely alignment with observed mean.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Meadow is the most-used anxiety protocol with strong results — most listeners report noticeable to moderate improvement with a balanced, comfortable rhythm that works well in therapy settings.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H-AX-L2 (Cottage, n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, meaningful confidence) performs slightly below L1 as expected. Distribution clusters at 7-8 (26/48) with limited upper tail but acceptable mid-range consistency. Qualitative data shows balanced settle_time modes (5 and 20 minutes), with 'Liked it' music opinion (9) and 'Balanced' rhythm (9). The moderate improvement signal is appropriate for a DESCENT protocol at this level.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage gets good marks for anxiety relief with a slightly quicker settling time than its sibling protocols. Listeners tend to like the music and find the rhythm balanced.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H-AX-L3 (Valley, n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, meaningful confidence) shows slight mean recovery over L2 but increased variance. Distribution has notable right tail (4 at 9, 5 at 10), suggesting a responder subgroup. Qualitative settle_time mode shifts to 'About 10 minutes' (10/20), consistent with longer protocols deepening entrainment more slowly. No YouTube data available.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley offers solid anxiety reduction that takes a bit longer to settle in — about 10 minutes — but many listeners report noticeable improvement by the end.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H-CR-L1 (Anchor, n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, meaningful confidence) performs as structurally expected for a crisis DESCENT protocol — appropriately lower given acute-distress entry. Distribution clusters at 6-8, matching crisis-state design intent. Qualitative data is encouraging: 'Significant improvement' leads change_rating (8/24), fast settle_time ('About 5 minutes' mode), and 'Liked it' music opinion (11). AutoGluon predicts 7.955 for crisis zone, notably above observed mean — worth tracking as n grows.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor is designed for acute crisis moments and delivers meaningful relief: many listeners report significant improvement and settle within about 5 minutes.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H-CR-L2 (Harbor, n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, meaningful confidence) shows nearly identical mean and variance to H-CR-L1, with slightly better pct_pos (55%). Qualitative data reveals a bimodal music opinion (Neutral: 6 vs. Loved it: 5) that does not appears to drive outcome variance. Settle_time is appropriately varied. AutoGluon predicts 7.955, the highest crisis-zone score, suggesting strong design potential.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor provides moderate crisis support with a balanced rhythm that some listeners love and others find just okay — the music opinion is split.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H-CR-L3 (Horizon, n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, meaningful confidence) matches L2 mean but with wider variance and unique distribution shape — clusters at 5-7 with sharp right tail (3 scores at 10). Qualitative settle_time mode is fastest in crisis family: 'Immediately / within first minute' (7/19), suggesting L3's longer duration enables earlier perceived entrainment onset. Elevated 'Not liked' music opinion (4) relative to other protocols warrants attention.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon works quickly — several listeners felt it immediately — though opinions on the music are more mixed than other crisis protocols.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H-SL-L1 (Lantern, n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, strong confidence) leads Sleep & Rest subfamily with wide but right-weighted distribution. Qualitative data (n=15) is thin but positive: 'Noticeable improvement' (6) and fast settle_time. AutoGluon predicts 7.539 and routes X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2 cross-bucket, suggesting sleep protocols share feature space with low-affect Mood & Energy states.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern is the top sleep protocol with most listeners reporting noticeable improvement and settling within about 5 minutes — a strong performer.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H-SL-L2 (Hammock, n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, meaningful confidence) shows lowest variance in Sleep subfamily — most reliable performer. Distribution concentrates at 7-8 (23/45). Qualitative listener_type mode is 'A client / patient' (6/15), with 'Neutral / so-so' music opinion (6). Professional-use skew may explain tight variance — clinicians assess more consistently than self-listeners.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock is used more by professionals with clients than self-listeners, and delivers consistent sleep support with balanced rhythm despite mixed music opinions.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H-SL-L3 (Midnight, n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, meaningful confidence) performs comparably to L2 with compact 6-9 distribution. Qualitative data shows 'Noticeable improvement' (7) and unique rhythm_opinion note: 'Slightly busy' (7) — the only Sleep protocol with this feedback. May indicate L3 groove density approaches discomfort threshold for sleep-seekers.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight helps with sleep but has slightly more listeners noting the rhythm feels slightly busy compared to other sleep protocols.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T-FC-L1 (Compass, n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, meaningful confidence) is weakest THRIVING result with distribution mass at 4-8. Qualitative reveals critical signal: 'rhythm_opinion: Slightly busy' (9) is top rhythm complaint in entire dataset. Despite this, change_rating is predominantly 'Noticeable improvement' (10). Groove density likely impedes entrainment depth for focus-seekers — this is the primary development signal for THRIVING.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass is the weakest focus protocol — the rhythm feels slightly busy to many listeners, which may be limiting how well it works for concentration.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T-FC-L2 (Waypoint, n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, meaningful confidence) shows substantial jump over L1 (+0.73) — largest level-to-level gain in dataset. Distribution peaks at 8 (10/32). Qualitative shows 'About 10 minutes' settle_time mode and split rhythm opinion. Appears to have resolved much of L1's groove friction.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint shows a big jump in effectiveness over Compass for focus, with most listeners noticing meaningful improvement.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T-FC-L3 (Summit, n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, meaningful confidence) is highest-scoring THRIVING protocol and strongest level-gradient signal. Distribution is starkly bimodal: 10 scores at 10 and 7 at 7-8, with minimal intermediate scoring — suggests population splitting. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (close to observed) and routes cross-bucket to X-ME-L1/X-MD-L1. YouTube views=15 highest in THRIVING but retention NULL.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit is the highest-performing focus protocol overall; many users report significant improvement though some experience much weaker results — a clear split in who benefits most.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T-CF-L1 (Ember, n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, meaningful confidence) opens Creative Flow HOLD family with mid-range distribution and moderate variance. Qualitative data shows split change_rating between 'Slight' and 'Significant' improvement, and bimodal settle_time ('Immediately' vs 'About 10 minutes'). This suggests HOLD arc produces varied entrainment onset depending on listener initiation state.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember is a creative flow protocol with mixed results — some listeners improve slightly, others significantly, and settling time varies a lot depending on the person.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T-CF-L2 (Current, n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, meaningful confidence) shows tightest variance across THRIVING protocols — most consistent mid-range performer. Distribution peaks at 7 (8/24), with no scores above 10. Qualitative music_opinion modes show high aesthetic approval ('Loved it': 6, 'Liked it': 6). Appears to deliver reliable but not breakout efficacy.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current delivers consistent but not outstanding creative flow support; listeners tend to love the music even if the improvement is moderate.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T-CF-L3 (Aurora, n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, developing confidence) is standout Creative Flow performer despite small n. Distribution is tight and right-shifted (5 at 7, 5 at 8, no scores below 5). Qualitative (n=25) shows balanced 5-10 minute settle_time and 'Balanced / comfortable' rhythm (14). Small n tempers confidence but signal is strong for L3 deepening effect.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora stands out as the strongest creative flow protocol with high improvement ratings and a comfortable rhythm that helps many users.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T-MB-L1 (Stone, n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, meaningful confidence) shows lowest variance in Maintenance subfamily with tight 6-9 distribution, no scores below 5. Qualitative shows slow settle_time mode ('About 20 minutes': 9/23) and high 'Therapy session' activity (8/23) — most clinically-oriented THRIVING usage. Designed for slow, deep entrainment in professional settings.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone is used heavily in therapy sessions with slow settling times — it takes about 20 minutes for most listeners but delivers consistent maintenance benefits.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T-MB-L2 (Axis, n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, meaningful confidence) shows highest variance in subfamily with broad 2-10 distribution. Rhythm opinion split: 'Slightly busy' (7) vs 'Balanced' (7). Suggests groove resonance mismatch for approximately half the listener base.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis shows varied results with split opinions on rhythmbusyness — some find it balanced, others slightly busy, reflecting inconsistent experiences.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T-MB-L3 (Orbit, n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, developing confidence) has strong pct_pos (71%) but peculiar distribution: 7 scores at 7, gap, then 2 at 10. Qualitative flags tension: 'Did NOT settle' is second-place settle_time mode (4/19). Listeners who settle have strong experience but nontrivial subset finds protocol uncomfortable. AutoGluon predicts 7.693.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit has strong positive rates but a notable subset reports not settling at all, suggesting it works well for some but not everyone.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X-ME-L1 (Breeze, n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, meaningful confidence) shows moderate Mood & Energy efficacy with diverse listener mix. Qualitative shows mixed listener_type distribution ('Myself': 10, 'A client/patient': 6, 'Someone I support': 5) with 'Moderate improvement' leading change_rating (7). AutoGluon predicts high cross-bucket influence — appears in routing for 7 of 9 zones, suggesting broad entrainment utility. YouTube views=8 with NULL retention.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze provides moderate mood improvement and is used across different listener types including caregivers supporting others.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise, n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, meaningful confidence) performs slightly above L1 with more concentrated distribution. Qualitative shows 'Significant improvement' leading (7) and balanced settle_time modes. YouTube views=8, retention NULL. AutoGluon predicts cross-bucket routing influence for crisis, sleep, and maintenance zones.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise shows stronger improvement signals with many listeners reporting significant positive change despite moderate overall scores.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X-ME-L3 (Updraft, n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, developing confidence) shows declined efficacy from L2 despite higher level. Distribution clusters mid-range. Qualitative shows 'Noticeable improvement' (9) leading but notably 'No effect' (5) — highest no-effect proportion in dataset. May indicate that L3's ASCENT arc overshoots for certain listener subgroups.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft has the most mixed results in mood protocols — while some notice improvement, a notable group reports no effect at all.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X-MD-L1 (Footpath, n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, meaningful confidence) opens ASCENT Motivation & Drive family with expected challenge: pct_pos only 45%. Distribution stretches 3-10, indicating wide ASCENT variability. Balanced qualitative signals with 'Noticeable improvement' leading. AutoGluon predicts 7.876, substantially above observed — indicates high design potential undercurrent.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath is designed to increase motivation but only about half of listeners report improvement — the lowest positive rate, indicating ASCENT protocols face real challenges.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X-MD-L2 (Trail, n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, developing confidence) descends further with pct_pos dropping to 43% — lowest in dataset. Distribution clusters 4-7 with no scores above 8. Qualitative settle_time is slow ('About 20 minutes': 5/21), suggesting many listeners not completing entrainment. ASCENT arc may be too gradual for L2 duration.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail has the lowest positive rate in the dataset (43%) with slow settling — the motivation boost seems to take longer than many listeners expect.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X-MD-L3 (Switchback, n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, developing confidence) defies sibling gradient entirely with substantial jump to 7.15 vs. 6.36 (L1) and 6.21 (L2). Distribution shows right tail with 3 scores at 9 and 2 at 10. Qualitative shows 'Moderate improvement' (8) leading and 'About 5 minutes' settle_time (5). This protocol has found an effective ASCENT configuration worth investigating for L1/L2 re-tuning.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback bucks the trend by actually working well for motivation despite its siblings struggling — many report moderate to noticeable improvement.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X-MC-L1 (Cavern, n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, meaningful confidence) opens Mind & Clarity DESCENT family with competent but unremarkable distribution. Qualitative includes rare 'It actually made things worse' (2/25) — only explicit negative change_rating across protocols with qualitative data. Otherwise balanced.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern provides decent mind clarity for most, though two listeners reported it actually made things worse — a rare negative signal worth noting.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X-MC-L2 (Starlight, n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, developing confidence) shows slight improvement over L1 with distribution concentrated 5-8. Qualitative is strong: 'Significant improvement' leads (7/20), and 'Very settling / calming' dominates rhythm (12/20). Appears to have found groove resonance L1 missed. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 and routes cross-bucket to X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight shows strong significant improvement and very settling rhythm, standing out as the most calming in the Mind & Clarity series.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos, n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, developing confidence) is the most concerning signal in the dataset — lowest mean, highest variance, nearly flat distribution 2-9. Qualitative shows rhythm split ('Very settling' vs 'Too busy': 6 vs 4), suggesting groove incompatibility. Small n demands caution but combination of low mean, high variance, and conflicting qualitative warrants priority investigation.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos has the weakest results in the entire dataset with high variability — some love it, others don't, and the rhythm opinion is split between very settling and too busy.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The dataset shows robust early efficacy across all series, with HEALING and THRIVING performing comparably (means 6.98 and 6.96) and TRANSFORMING trailing slightly (mean 6.59) as expected given its ascending-arc design.
• Sample sizes range from n=10 to n=95, with strong confidence (n≥50) in 8 HEALING protocols and 1 THRIVING protocol.
• YouTube reach is minimal across all protocols (views: 2-30), limiting retention signal interpretation.
• AutoGluon identifies avg_view_percentage and avg_view_duration as the strongest predictors (combined importance 0.50), highlighting the critical role of engagement depth.
• Cross-bucket routing suggests X-ME-L1 has outsized influence on predicted outcomes across zones, indicating potential design leverage point.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series efficacy profiles generally align with their therapeutic architecture, though with meaningful internal variation.
• HEALING protocols demonstrate consistent mid-range efficacy (mean 6.98, std 1.77), with crisis protocols (H-CR-*) appropriately underperforming relative toAnxiety/Over-stimulation/Sleep entry points.
• THRIVING shows the strongest internal level-gradient effect in Focus & Clarity (T-FC-L1: 6.46 → T-FC-L3: 7.63), with Creative Flow and Maintenance protocols performing at medium-high efficacy.
• TRANSFORMING protocols carry the widest variance (std 1.87), with ASCENT Motivation & Drive protocols showing the lowest pct_pos (43-62%) and the highest cross-run analytical drift, suggesting the ascending arc presents genuine entrainment challenges.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols exhibit distributional patterns that warrant investigation as potential subgroup effects or design mismatches.
• T-FC-L3 shows pronounced bimodality with 10 scores at 10 and the mode at 7-8, suggesting a population split between profound focus achievers and plateau responders.
• X-MC-L3 is the most concerning outlier: lowest mean (5.70), highest variance (std 2.45), and a nearly flat distribution from 2-9 with only n=10 — this combination warrants priority investigation.
• X-MD-L3 defies its sibling gradient with mean 7.15 vs. 6.36 (L1) and 6.21 (L2), suggesting the L3 ASCENT implementation has found an effective configuration that L1/L2 lack.
• T-MB-L3 shows tension between strong pct_pos (71%) and a notable 'Did NOT settle' response mode (4/19), indicating some listeners experience clear benefit while others find the protocol uncomfortable.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Concrete protocol-level findings point to several iterative improvement opportunities.
• T-FC-L1 (Compass, n=41) is the lowest THRIVING performer with a distinctive 'Slightly busy' rhythm complaint from 9/23 respondents — the most common rhythm criticism in the dataset. Consider reducing groove density for focus-seeking listeners.
[CONVERGENT SIGNAL] • X-MC-L3 (Cosmos, n=10, mean 5.70, std 2.45) warrants priority design review: the combination of lowest mean, highest variance, conflicting rhythm opinions (Very settling vs. Too busy), and small n suggests the DESCENT arc may be misaligned for its low-affect entry state.
• X-MD-L3 shows unexpectedly strong ASCENT performance (mean 7.15 vs. siblings at 6.36 and 6.21) — investigate the specific Hz trajectory and WING RATIO as a template for potential L1/L2 re-tuning.
• H-OS-L1 has the highest YouTube reach (views=30) but no retention data — prioritize adding avg_view_percentage tracking to understand whether high reach translates to entrainment completion.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding analysis reveals significant analytical drift concentrated in TRANSFORMING protocols and an unusually tight semantic cluster around Over-stimulation protocols.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: All five X-MD-* protocols show cosine similarity below 0.65 (range 0.507-0.647), indicating the analytical framing for Motivation & Drive is actively evolving — likely data-driven. X-ME-L2 and X-ME-L3 also show notable drift.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: H-OS-L1/L2/L3 form the tightest cluster in the dataset (pairwise sim 0.688-0.786), with H-OS-L1 being the most analytically distinct protocol (mean_sim=0.714).
• Confidence note: Drift metrics are based on 4-5 runs per protocol over 2-3 months — more historical data is needed to distinguish genuine pattern shifts from early analytical instability.

Appears to deliver reliable but not breakout efficacy.
by_protocol.T-CF-L3: T-CF-L3 (Aurora, n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, developing confidence) is standout Creative Flow performer despite small n. Distribution is tight and right-shifted (5 at 7, 5 at 8, no scores below 5). Qualitative (n=25) shows balanced 5-10 minute settle_time and 'Balanced / comfortable' rhythm (14). Small n tempers confidence but signal is strong for L3 deepening effect.
by_protocol.T-FC-L1: T-FC-L1 (Compass, n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, meaningful confidence) is weakest THRIVING result with distribution mass at 4-8. Qualitative reveals critical signal: 'rhythm_opinion: Slightly busy' (9) is top rhythm complaint in entire dataset. Despite this, change_rating is predominantly 'Noticeable improvement' (10). Groove density likely impedes entrainment depth for focus-seekers — this is the primary development signal for THRIVING.
by_protocol.T-FC-L2: T-FC-L2 (Waypoint, n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, meaningful confidence) shows substantial jump over L1 (+0.73) — largest level-to-level gain in dataset. Distribution peaks at 8 (10/32). Qualitative shows 'About 10 minutes' settle_time mode and split rhythm opinion. Appears to have resolved much of L1's groove friction.
by_protocol.T-FC-L3: T-FC-L3 (Summit, n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, meaningful confidence) is highest-scoring THRIVING protocol and strongest level-gradient signal. Distribution is starkly bimodal: 10 scores at 10 and 7 at 7-8, with minimal intermediate scoring — suggests population splitting. AutoGluon predicts 7.728 (close to observed) and routes cross-bucket to X-ME-L1/X-MD-L1. YouTube views=15 highest in THRIVING but retention NULL.
by_protocol.T-MB-L1: T-MB-L1 (Stone, n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, meaningful confidence) shows lowest variance in Maintenance subfamily with tight 6-9 distribution, no scores below 5. Qualitative shows slow settle_time mode ('About 20 minutes': 9/23) and high 'Therapy session' activity (8/23) — most clinically-oriented THRIVING usage. Designed for slow, deep entrainment in professional settings.
by_protocol.T-MB-L2: T-MB-L2 (Axis, n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, meaningful confidence) shows highest variance in subfamily with broad 2-10 distribution. Rhythm opinion split: 'Slightly busy' (7) vs 'Balanced' (7). Suggests groove resonance mismatch for approximately half the listener base.
by_protocol.T-MB-L3: T-MB-L3 (Orbit, n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, developing confidence) has strong pct_pos (71%) but peculiar distribution: 7 scores at 7, gap, then 2 at 10. Qualitative flags tension: 'Did NOT settle' is second-place settle_time mode (4/19). Listeners who settle have strong experience but nontrivial subset finds protocol uncomfortable. AutoGluon predicts 7.693.
by_protocol.X-MC-L1: X-MC-L1 (Cavern, n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, meaningful confidence) opens Mind & Clarity DESCENT family with competent but unremarkable distribution. Qualitative includes rare 'It actually made things worse' (2/25) — only explicit negative change_rating across protocols with qualitative data. Otherwise balanced.
by_protocol.X-MC-L2: X-MC-L2 (Starlight, n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, developing confidence) shows slight improvement over L1 with distribution concentrated 5-8. Qualitative is strong: 'Significant improvement' leads (7/20), and 'Very settling / calming' dominates rhythm (12/20). Appears to have found groove resonance L1 missed. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 and routes cross-bucket to X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos, n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, developing confidence) is the most concerning signal in the dataset — lowest mean, highest variance, nearly flat distribution 2-9. Qualitative shows rhythm split ('Very settling' vs 'Too busy': 6 vs 4), suggesting groove incompatibility. Small n demands caution but combination of low mean, high variance, and conflicting qualitative warrants priority investigation.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: X-MD-L1 (Footpath, n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, meaningful confidence) opens ASCENT Motivation & Drive family with expected challenge: pct_pos only 45%. Distribution stretches 3-10, indicating wide ASCENT variability. Balanced qualitative signals with 'Noticeable improvement' leading. AutoGluon predicts 7.876, substantially above observed — indicates high design potential undercurrent.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: X-MD-L2 (Trail, n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, developing confidence) descends further with pct_pos dropping to 43% — lowest in dataset. Distribution clusters 4-7 with no scores above 8. Qualitative settle_time is slow ('About 20 minutes': 5/21), suggesting many listeners not completing entrainment. ASCENT arc may be too gradual for L2 duration.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: X-MD-L3 (Switchback, n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, developing confidence) defies sibling gradient entirely with substantial jump to 7.15 vs. 6.36 (L1) and 6.21 (L2). Distribution shows right tail with 3 scores at 9 and 2 at 10. Qualitative shows 'Moderate improvement' (8) leading and 'About 5 minutes' settle_time (5). This protocol has found an effective ASCENT configuration worth investigating for L1/L2 re-tuning.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: X-ME-L1 (Breeze, n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, meaningful confidence) shows moderate Mood & Energy efficacy with diverse listener mix. Qualitative shows mixed listener_type distribution ('Myself': 10, 'A client/patient': 6, 'Someone I support': 5) with 'Moderate improvement' leading change_rating (7). AutoGluon predicts high cross-bucket influence — appears in routing for 7 of 9 zones, suggesting broad entrainment utility. YouTube views=8 with NULL retention.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise, n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, meaningful confidence) performs slightly above L1 with more concentrated distribution. Qualitative shows 'Significant improvement' leading (7) and balanced settle_time modes. YouTube views=8, retention NULL. AutoGluon predicts cross-bucket routing influence for crisis, sleep, and maintenance zones.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: X-ME-L3 (Updraft, n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, developing confidence) shows declined efficacy from L2 despite higher level. Distribution clusters mid-range. Qualitative shows 'Noticeable improvement' (9) leading but notably 'No effect' (5) — highest no-effect proportion in dataset. May indicate that L3's ASCENT arc overshoots for certain listener subgroups.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Meadow is the most-used anxiety protocol with strong results — most listeners report noticeable to moderate improvement with a balanced, comfortable rhythm that works well in therapy settings.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Cottage gets good marks for anxiety relief with a slightly quicker settling time than its sibling protocols. Listeners tend to like the music and find the rhythm balanced.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Valley offers solid anxiety reduction that takes a bit longer to settle in — about 10 minutes — but many listeners report noticeable improvement by the end.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Anchor is designed for acute crisis moments and delivers meaningful relief: many listeners report significant improvement and settle within about 5 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Harbor provides moderate crisis support with a balanced rhythm that some listeners love and others find just okay — the music opinion is split.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Horizon works quickly — several listeners felt it immediately — though opinions on the music are more mixed than other crisis protocols.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Pebble is the most popular over-stimulation protocol with strong results; most listeners notice improvement with a balanced, calming rhythm that works for many activities.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Rainfall offers reliable over-stimulation relief with noticeable improvement and a comfortable rhythm, though it's not widely discovered yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Glacier has solid performance and seems to work especially well for people using it to fall asleep — the rhythm is balanced with a very settling quality for many.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Lantern is the top sleep protocol with most listeners reporting noticeable improvement and settling within about 5 minutes — a strong performer.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Hammock is used more by professionals with clients than self-listeners, and delivers consistent sleep support with balanced rhythm despite mixed music opinions.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Midnight helps with sleep but has slightly more listeners noting the rhythm feels slightly busy compared to other sleep protocols.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Ember is a creative flow protocol with mixed results — some listeners improve slightly, others significantly, and settling time varies a lot depending on the person.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Current delivers consistent but not outstanding creative flow support; listeners tend to love the music even if the improvement is moderate.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Aurora stands out as the strongest creative flow protocol with high improvement ratings and a comfortable rhythm that helps many users.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Compass is the weakest focus protocol — the rhythm feels slightly busy to many listeners, which may be limiting how well it works for concentration.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Waypoint shows a big jump in effectiveness over Compass for focus, with most listeners noticing meaningful improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Summit is the highest-performing focus protocol overall; many users report significant improvement though some experience much weaker results — a clear split in who benefits most.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Stone is used heavily in therapy sessions with slow settling times — it takes about 20 minutes for most listeners but delivers consistent maintenance benefits.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Axis shows varied results with split opinions on rhythmbusyness — some find it balanced, others slightly busy, reflecting inconsistent experiences.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Orbit has strong positive rates but a notable subset reports not settling at all, suggesting it works well for some but not everyone.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Cavern provides decent mind clarity for most, though two listeners reported it actually made things worse — a rare negative signal worth noting.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Starlight shows strong significant improvement and very settling rhythm, standing out as the most calming in the Mind & Clarity series.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Cosmos has the weakest results in the entire dataset with high variability — some love it, others don't, and the rhythm opinion is split between very settling and too busy.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Footpath is designed to increase motivation but only about half of listeners report improvement — the lowest positive rate, indicating ASCENT protocols face real challenges.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Trail has the lowest positive rate in the dataset (43%) with slow settling — the motivation boost seems to take longer than many listeners expect.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Switchback bucks the trend by actually working well for motivation despite its siblings struggling — many report moderate to noticeable improvement.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Breeze provides moderate mood improvement and is used across different listener types including caregivers supporting others.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Sunrise shows stronger improvement signals with many listeners reporting significant positive change despite moderate overall scores.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Updraft has the most mixed results in mood protocols — while some notice improvement, a notable group reports no effect at all.
